lesson.menu_desc: Arranging to meet up with friends in Paris -- present continuous for future + Let's / Why don't we...? -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Making Plans with Friends
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gabriela Pires | Aula 19 -- Making Plans with Friends | Alumni by Better

listenings.text: Hi Gabriela, it's Lucas! I'm calling about this weekend. So, a few of us are getting together on Saturday afternoon. We're meeting at the cafe downtown at four o'clock, the one near the park. After that, we're going to the movies at six. I'm really looking forward to it. I know you are busy with your French class in the morning, but are you free in the afternoon? Why don't you come with us? Let's hang out, it's going to be so much fun. Oh, and please be on time, because the movie starts early. Send me a message and tell me your plan. Talk soon, bye!

This is my plan for Saturday. In the morning I'm not free, because I have my French class. But in the afternoon I'm meeting my friends downtown. We're getting together at the cafe near the park at four o'clock. Lucas is coming too, and I'm really looking forward to it. After the cafe, we're going to the movies at six, so I need to be on time. In the evening I'm not busy, so maybe we will hang out a little more. I love making plans with my friends in English. It feels great to say where I'm going and what I'm doing this weekend.

extra_audio.text: First, you send a message to your friend. Next, you suggest a time and you say, let's meet at four. After that, your friend asks, where are we getting together? Then, you agree on a place and you say, we're meeting at the cafe downtown. Finally, you confirm the plan and you say, see you on Saturday, don't be late.
